subject: An armored inquisitor in dark plate armor stands holding a long sword point-down, with luminous white feathered angelic wings spreading from their back; the wings are the titular Lunarch Mantle, a holy aura granting flight.
description: A grim, dark-armored figure stands at center frame, gripping a long straight sword point-down before them in a sentinel pose. From their shoulders unfurl massive luminous white-feathered wings that catch a cold celestial light, framing the figure like a cathedral arch. The armor is matte black with engraved or fluted plates and a high collar; the figure's face is in deep shadow, indistinguishable. The background is near-pitch black with faint ambient haze, throwing the wings and sword into stark relief. The flavor text reads 'A boon from the angels should never be cast aside,' attributed to Manfried Ulnach, Chief Inquisitor.
foreground: armored figure with sword and angelic wings
background: near-black void with faint cold haze
emotion: stoic, resolute, severe — the body language is sentinel-still and judgmental
